mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount rates on equity cash flows decline and risk appetite rises, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directional only if the easing path is already visible in the monthly series.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25 pp over 3 months but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recessionary threshold indicates weakening income growth and higher default risk, which should pressure equities lower over the next month. The effect is strongest once unemployment is already elevated, not during low-unemployment expansions.
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or the average forward return is not below the unconditional sample mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both improve simultaneously, it signals stronger housing demand and consumer spending, which should support equities over a one-quarter horizon. The interaction matters because one series alone can reflect noise, but joint strength indicates broad demand momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS does not outperform each series used alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-strength observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO relative to prior months signals manufacturing contraction and weaker corporate revenue growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when production weakness is offset by aggressive policy easing.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the average return is not negative after accounting for the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A steep fall in RSAFS indicates soft retail spending and deteriorating near-term corporate sales, which should weigh on equities over the next week. The effect is short-horizon because sales data are a direct read on current demand momentum.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if monthly RSAFS declines greater than 1.0% are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates become more negative and financial conditions ease, supporting equities over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires inflation pressure without offsetting rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3m annualized inflation > FEDFUNDS by at least 2.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy gap exceeds 2.0 pp but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, or if the interaction adds no incremental explanatory power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS together with a rising UNRATE signals a genuine labor-market deterioration that should depress equity returns over the following quarter. The mechanism is stronger than either series alone because it identifies both job losses and higher slack.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered windows, or if the joint effect is not stronger than each individual series.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, rising GDPC1 and HOUST indicate broad real activity reacceleration, which should support equity prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-specific because growth surprises matter more after output has been weak.
failure_condition: Reject if, during post-recession recovery periods, simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and HOUST does not lead to positive SPX 20-day returns more often than negative ones, or if conditional rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 2-year window.
